allowed_claim: cuFlye can consume the M6b replay pack with a standalone CUDA raw-overlap filter/sort prototype and reproduce the selected chain_input oracle exactly.
forbidden_claim: M6c does not implement quickSeqOverlaps, minimizer bucket generation, Flye graph consumption, default GPU mode, or whole-Flye acceleration.
next_recommended_step: M6d: define a richer read-to-graph minimizer-source pack with query sequence, graph edge sequences, VertexIndex buckets, and k-mer/minimizer parameters so CUDA can move from raw-overlap replay toward true candidate/minimizer generation.
plain_language_benefit: M6c is the first CUDA step at the M6 input-boundary, but it still does not make Flye faster. It proves a CUDA kernel can consume the new M6b pack and emit exactly the same 8 chain_input rows as CPU replay. On this tiny pack the total CUDA process path is slower because startup/context overhead dominates; the useful gain is correctness and a governed CUDA output format.
